steps.title: Translate the total shrub constraint
steps.content: The phrase no more than 244 total shrubs means the sum cannot exceed 244. The phrase no more than translates to the inequality symbol ≤. Therefore, the total number of shrubs is: $$h + w \le 244$$ This eliminates choices C and D, which use ≥ instead.

steps.title: Translate the hydrangea-to-hazel relationship
steps.content:
The phrase the number of hydrangeas will be at most three times the number of winter hazels means hydrangeas cannot exceed 3 times the hazel count.

The phrase at most translates to ≤. Therefore: $$h \le 3w$$ Choice B has $3h \ge w$ (equivalent to $h \ge \frac{w}{3}$), which says hydrangeas are at least one-third the hazel count — the opposite of what we need.

Choice D also has this reversed relationship. Only choice A has both $h + w \le 244$ and $h \le 3w$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
